channel.title: Cadence 步频 — PT Research Signal
channel.description: Daily curated physical therapy & rehab research for clinicians. AI-scored signal from 50+ journals.

item.title: Early versus conventional postoperative rehabilitation after reverse total shoulder arthroplasty: a systematic review of functional outcomes and complications.
item.description: A systematic review found that early rehabilitation (motion ≤14 days) after primary reverse total shoulder arthroplasty (rTSA) showed comparable functional outcomes and complication rates to delayed programs (motion ≥28 days).
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Dry needling and muscle mechanical properties in myofascial trigger points: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis evaluated the effects of dry needling on myofascial mechanical properties in adults with active or latent myofascial trigger points, confirming improvements.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Efficacy of High-Intensity Laser Therapy for Meralgia Paresthetica: A Randomized, Double-Blind, Sham-Controlled Trial.
item.description: A randomized, double-blind, sham-controlled trial (N=54 analyzed) found 12 sessions of High-Intensity Laser Therapy (HILT) significantly improved pain (Numeric Rating Scale: -6.4 vs -1.8, p<0.001) and function (MPQ-S: -11.9 vs -3.5, p<0.001) compared to sham in Meralgia Paresthetica (MP) patients at 12 weeks.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Investigation of the Effectiveness of Extracorporeal Shock Wave Therapy and Kinesiotaping in Individuals With Carpal Tunnel Syndrome: A Randomized Controlled Trial.
item.description: A randomized controlled trial of 54 individuals with mild to moderate carpal tunnel syndrome found extracorporeal shock wave therapy and kinesiotaping, combined with splinting and exercises, to be effective.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Bone-anchored prostheses for transtibial amputation: A systematic review of outcomes, complications, and cost-effectiveness
item.description: A systematic review on bone-anchored prostheses for transtibial amputation examined outcomes, complications, and cost-effectiveness, revealing varying etiologies globally.
item.author: Archives of PM&R

item.title: Exercise interventions for rotator cuff-related shoulder pain in middle-aged and older adults: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis found exercise-based interventions to be effective for pain, function, range of motion, and strength in middle-aged and older adults with rotator cuff-related shoulder pain.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Effectiveness of physical therapy techniques and methods in the management of endometriosis symptoms: A systematic review with meta-analysis.
item.description: A systematic review with meta-analysis evaluated the effectiveness of different physical therapy methods and techniques in improving symptoms triggered by endometriosis.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: "Comparison Between Robotic and Conventional Walking Recovery Training Methods in People With Spinal Cord Injury": Systematic Review with Pairwise and Network meta-analysis.
item.description: A systematic review and network meta-analysis of 16 studies compared Robotic Body Weight Supported Treadmill Training (RBWSTT), Overground Robotic Exoskeleton Training (ORET), and Conventional Physiotherapy (CP) on gait and cardiovascular outcomes in individuals with Spinal Cord Injury (SCI).
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Community-Based Exercise for Chronic Pain in People with Spinal Cord Injury: A Pragmatic Randomized Controlled Trial
item.description: A pragmatic randomized controlled trial tested the hypothesis that home/community-based exercise, prescribed according to the Spinal Cord Injury (SCI) Exercise Guidelines, reduces chronic pain.
item.author: Archives of PM&R

item.title: Using visual biofeedback to reduce step length error at fast walking speeds is feasible after stroke
item.description: A study on medRxiv suggests that using visual biofeedback to reduce step length error at fast walking speeds is feasible for individuals after stroke.
item.author: medRxiv

item.title: Efficacy of repetitive transcranial magnetic stimulation combined with soft robotic glove training for slight-to-moderate post-stroke upper limb spasticity: a randomized controlled trial.
item.description: 一项随机对照试验（N=54）显示，重复经颅磁刺激（rTMS）联合软性机器人手套训练能改善轻中度卒中后上肢痉挛患者的腕屈肌痉挛和上肢运动功能。
item.author: PubMed

item.title: The impact of a brief online education intervention on the knowledge, attitudes and beliefs of qualified physiotherapists about chronic low back pain: Pre- and post-intervention survey.
item.description: A pre-post intervention survey showed a brief online education program improved qualified physiotherapists' knowledge, attitudes, and beliefs about chronic low back pain, shifting towards biopsychosocial approaches.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: The Effect of Duration of Supervised Rehabilitation on Outcomes Following Anterior Cruciate Ligament Reconstruction: A Systematic Review.
item.description: A systematic review explored the effect of supervised rehabilitation duration on outcomes following anterior cruciate ligament reconstruction (ACLR), aiming to improve outcomes and reduce re-injury risk.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Lung Boost Trainer Versus Incentive Spirometer on Pulmonary Function Post COVID Hemiplegic Cerebral Palsy Children; Randomized Controlled Trial.
item.description: A randomized controlled trial compared the efficacy of Lung Boost Trainer versus incentive spirometer on pulmonary function in children with COVID hemiplegic cerebral palsy.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Sex and gender differences in technology-based rehabilitation for people with stroke: A scoping review.
item.description: A scoping review mapped evidence on sex and gender differences in technology-assisted stroke rehabilitation, identifying significant gaps and highlighting the need for equitable approaches.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: From rehabilitation to performance: reframing vestibular-ocular interventions to optimise sports performance.
item.description: This article discusses reframing vestibular-ocular interventions to optimize sports performance, moving beyond traditional rehabilitation contexts.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: "Are Cardiac Rehabilitation Programs Effective in Improving Aerobic Capacity for People with Transient Ischemic Attack and Stroke? A Rapid Review and Meta-analysis".
item.description: A rapid review and meta-analysis of nine studies (N=490) found that exercise-based cardiac rehabilitation was effective in improving aerobic capacity for people with stroke or transient ischemic attack (TIA).
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Short-term relaxation after cervical rotatory manipulation is more closely associated with somatosensory input than cracking sound: a randomized controlled EEG study
item.description: A randomized controlled EEG study found that short-term relaxation after cervical rotatory manipulation is more closely associated with somatosensory input than the cracking sound.
item.author: medRxiv

item.title: Accuracy of visual estimation of shoulder abduction by expert physiotherapists: a comparative study using 3D motion capture.
item.description: A comparative study found that expert physiotherapists' visual estimation of shoulder abduction, even with video assistance, showed only moderate to good agreement (ICC 0.70-0.84) with 3D motion capture, suggesting potential inaccuracies in clinical practice.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Between the Snap and the Return: A Prospective Narrative Analysis of Four Voices from inside Anterior Cruciate Ligament Reconstruction Recovery.
item.description: A prospective narrative analysis explored four patients' evolving experiences throughout anterior cruciate ligament reconstruction recovery.
item.author: PubMed
